title: cyperus ferax var. squarrosus
intro: Cyperus ferax var. squarrosus, also known as squarrosus cyperus, is a type of flowering plant in the sedge family (Cyperaceae) characterized by its unique, squarrose (bristly) leaf tips. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Cyperus ferax var. squarrosus are attributed to its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and exhibit antioxidant properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is often dried and used as a tea or tincture, or its roots are used in infusions for various health applications.
